nodes.name: Config
nodes.notes: Creators API v1: data source is the Amazon Creators API (PA-API successor for affiliates), OAuth2 client-credentials — NO AWS SigV4, NO refresh token. ⚠️ creatorsCredentialId/creatorsCredentialSecret are the Credential ID + Secret created in Associates Central → Tools → Creators API, stored HERE (secret-in-file tradeoff: Code nodes can't read the credentials manager and $env is unavailable on this plan). Account must be approved (≥10 qualifying sales / 30 days). Token: POST https://{creatorsTokenHost}/auth/o2/token (grant_type=client_credentials, scope=creatorsapi::default). API: POST https://{creatorsHost}/catalog/v1/searchItems with Authorization: Bearer + x-marketplace={creatorsMarketplace}. Discovery = searchItems by creatorsKeywords; price/images/RATINGS come from the search response (offersV2/images/customerReviews) — one call, no separate pricing node. amazonAffiliateTag = PartnerTag. OpenRouter + S3 keys still use n8n credentials.

nodes.name: Fetch Categories
nodes.notes: One row per category. Reused via $('Fetch Categories').all() in Build Classifier Request.

nodes.name: Fetch Subcategories
nodes.notes: NEW: Loads subcategories table so AI-generated subcategory name can be resolved to subcategory_id (uuid). Adjust column names if schema differs.

nodes.name: Get Creators Token
nodes.notes: Creators API: OAuth2 client-credentials token. POST https://{creatorsTokenHost}/auth/o2/token with grant_type=client_credentials, scope=creatorsapi::default, creatorsCredentialId/Secret in the body → access_token. No refresh token (client-credentials). Throws on invalid_client/invalid_scope/unauthorized_client (latter = account not approved). If a 400 content-type error appears, switch the body to JSON.

nodes.name: Search Items
nodes.notes: Creators API: POST https://{creatorsHost}/catalog/v1/searchItems with Authorization: Bearer + x-marketplace. lowerCamelCase PA-API resources incl. offersV2.listings.price (+savingBasis), images.primary.large, customerReviews (ratings). Returns searchResult.items. Replaces the SP-API catalog+pricing pair — price/images/ratings now come from this single call.

nodes.name: Extract Items
nodes.notes: Creators API: emits { asin, item } per product (capped at maxDealsPerRun). The item already carries price (offersV2), images, and customerReviews from the search response — no separate pricing call.

nodes.name: Loop Over Items
nodes.notes: Output 0 = done (end of run). Output 1 = loop body, processes one PA-API item ({ asin, item }) at a time → Normalise.

nodes.name: Normalise
nodes.notes: SP-API v1: maps an SP-API Catalog Items item (read from Loop Over Items: { asin, item }) → product shape. name/brand from summaries[0]; price from the injected item._price (Get Competitive Pricing); image from images[0].images (MAIN/largest, with width/height); tags from summaries[0].browseClassification. rating=0, features=[], original_price=null, discount=0 (SP-API has no ratings, feature bullets, or list price). affiliate_url CLEAN/tagless. Price-less items borrow the run's FIRST valid price (priceFallback); first-item-with-no-price is skipped. valid=false → 'Is Valid?' false-branch → Log Skipped Item → loop continues. Output shape identical to the ScraperAPI version, so downstream is unchanged.

nodes.name: Is Valid?
nodes.notes: v3.1: replaces the old Filter (which DROPPED invalid items and stalled the loop — the loop-back edge never fired, halting the run). TRUE (valid) → Check Existing AI Fields. FALSE (skipReason set) → Log Skipped Item → back to Loop Over Items, so the queue always advances.

nodes.name: Log Skipped Item
nodes.notes: v3.1: NEW. Structured 'item_skipped' log (asin + reason + timestamp) for auditing, then loops back to Loop Over Items.

parameters.query:
-- v3.2: single-row seed LEFT JOIN-ed to products so this ALWAYS returns exactly
-- one row. A new product (no match) yields found=false and every has_*=false —
-- previously WHERE slug=$1 returned 0 rows, the node emitted no items, and the
-- whole loop body halted at this node.
SELECT
  (p.slug IS NOT NULL)                                                       AS found,
  COALESCE(p.subcategory_id IS NOT NULL, false)                              AS has_subcategory_id,
  COALESCE(array_length(p.pros, 1) > 0, false)                               AS has_pros,
  COALESCE(array_length(p.cons, 1) > 0, false)                               AS has_cons,
  COALESCE(p.seo_title IS NOT NULL AND p.seo_title <> '', false)             AS has_seo_title,
  COALESCE(p.seo_description IS NOT NULL AND p.seo_description <> '', false)  AS has_seo_description
FROM (SELECT $1::text AS slug) q
LEFT JOIN products p ON p.slug = q.slug;

nodes.name: Check Existing AI Fields
nodes.notes: v3.2: query rewritten to ALWAYS return exactly one row (single-row seed LEFT JOIN products) so new products no longer halt the loop here. found=false + has_*=false for new products → Compute AI Gate sets skipAi=false → AI runs. alwaysOutputData:true is an extra safety net.

nodes.name: Compute AI Gate
nodes.notes: NEW: Merges DB-populated check + overrideAiFields flag into a single skipAi boolean.

nodes.name: AI Fields Gate
nodes.notes: NEW: true-output (skipAi=false) → AI enrichment branch. false-output (skipAi=true) → bypass branch.

nodes.name: Build AI Enrichment Request
nodes.notes: NEW: Sends available subcategory names in prompt so AI picks an exact match. pros/cons requested as arrays, not strings.

nodes.name: Call OpenRouter (AI Enrichment)
nodes.notes: v3.3: Authorization injected by the predefined 'OpenRouter API' credential (paste API key only — n8n adds 'Bearer <key>'). You MUST create this credential and SELECT it on this node after import, else OpenRouter returns 401 'No auth credentials found' and pros/cons/seo come back empty. neverError:true captures 429/4xx bodies (now surfaced by Parse & Validate). 4 retries @ 3 s.

nodes.name: Parse & Validate AI Fields
nodes.notes: NEW: pros/cons returned as string[] (text[] compatible). subcategory_id resolved via 3-tier fuzzy match against Fetch Subcategories rows. seo_title ≤60, seo_description ≤160 hard-enforced.

nodes.name: AI Skip (already populated)
nodes.notes: v3.2: False-branch of AI Fields Gate. Passes product through with existing DB values untouched (empty pros/cons/seo; the upsert's COALESCE/non-empty guards preserve DB values). Connects directly to Build Classifier Request.

nodes.name: Call OpenRouter
nodes.notes: v3.3: Authorization injected by the predefined 'OpenRouter API' credential (same one as the enrichment node — select it here too). Category classifier call. On 401 it falls back to defaultCategoryId.

nodes.name: Upload to S3
nodes.notes: v3: REPLACES the hand-rolled SigV4 Code node + HTTP PUT pair. Uploads the downloaded image binary (property `data`) to the S3-compatible bucket using the '4everland S3' credential — endpoint (https://endpoint.4everland.co), region, access key, and secret key all live in n8n's credentials manager. Object key = Resolve Category.imageKey (e.g. products/amazon/<ASIN>/main.jpg). If 4everland rejects virtual-host addressing, enable 'Force Path Style' on the credential. Fallback documented in n8n/README.md if the native node can't reach the endpoint.

nodes.name: Build Payload
nodes.notes: v2: pros/cons as string[], subcategory_id (uuid), seo_og_image (jsonb) from S3 key. All column names match DB schema exactly.

parameters.query:
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
-- Upsert Product  v2
--
-- Column mapping (all names match DB schema exactly):
--   pros / cons / features / tags  → text[]  (jsonb input → ARRAY(jsonb_array_elements_text(...)))
--   subcategory_id                 → uuid    (nullable)
--   seo_og_image                   → jsonb   (nullable)
--   seo_title / seo_description    → text    (DB-level length guard as safety net)
--
-- All values arrive via a single parameterised JSONB blob ($1) — no SQL injection risk.
-- $2 = defaultCategoryId used to decide whether to overwrite category_id on conflict.
-- ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
WITH input AS (
  SELECT * FROM jsonb_to_record($1::jsonb) AS r(
    slug                 text,
    title                text,
    brand                text,
    short_description    text,
    description          text,
    category_id          uuid,
    price                numeric,
    original_price       numeric,
    currency             text,
    discount_percent     int,
    deal_label           text,
    affiliate_source     text,
    amazon_affiliate_tag text,
    affiliate_url        text,
    rating               real,
    images               jsonb,
    features             jsonb,
    tags                 jsonb,
    pros                 jsonb,
    cons                 jsonb,
    subcategory_id       uuid,
    seo_title            text,
    seo_description      text,
    seo_og_image         jsonb
  )
)
INSERT INTO products (
  slug, title, brand, short_description, description,
  category_id, price, original_price, currency, discount_percent,
  deal_label, is_deal, affiliate_source, amazon_affiliate_tag, affiliate_url,
  rating, images, features, tags, pros, cons,
  subcategory_id, seo_title, seo_description, seo_og_image,
  published_at
)
SELECT
  slug, title, brand, short_description, description,
  category_id,
  price::numeric(10,2),
  original_price::numeric(10,2),
  currency::public.currency,
  discount_percent,
  deal_label,
  true,                                      -- is_deal
  affiliate_source::public.affiliate_source,
  amazon_affiliate_tag,
  affiliate_url,                             -- stored as-is: raw HTTPS amazon.com URL
  rating,
  images,
  -- text[] columns: convert jsonb array → postgres text[]
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(features))  AS features,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(tags))       AS tags,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(pros))       AS pros,
  ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(cons))       AS cons,
  -- uuid (nullable)
  subcategory_id,
  -- DB-level length guard (application already enforces, but belt-and-suspenders)
  left(seo_title, 60)                        AS seo_title,
  left(seo_description, 160)                 AS seo_description,
  seo_og_image,
  NOW()                                      AS published_at
FROM input
ON CONFLICT (slug) DO UPDATE SET
  title                = EXCLUDED.title,
  brand                = EXCLUDED.brand,
  short_description    = EXCLUDED.short_description,
  description          = EXCLUDED.description,
  price                = EXCLUDED.price,
  original_price       = EXCLUDED.original_price,
  currency             = EXCLUDED.currency,
  discount_percent     = EXCLUDED.discount_percent,
  deal_label           = EXCLUDED.deal_label,
  is_deal              = true,
  affiliate_url        = EXCLUDED.affiliate_url,
  amazon_affiliate_tag = EXCLUDED.amazon_affiliate_tag,
  rating               = EXCLUDED.rating,
  -- JSONB array: only replace if incoming has items
  images               = CASE WHEN jsonb_array_length(EXCLUDED.images) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.images ELSE products.images END,
  seo_og_image         = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.seo_og_image, products.seo_og_image),
  -- text[] columns: only replace if incoming array is non-empty
  features             = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.features, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.features ELSE products.features END,
  tags                 = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.tags, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.tags     ELSE products.tags     END,
  -- pros / cons: COALESCE preserves existing populated values when AI was skipped
  pros                 = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.pros, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.pros     ELSE products.pros     END,
  cons                 = CASE WHEN array_length(EXCLUDED.cons, 1) > 0
                               THEN EXCLUDED.cons     ELSE products.cons     END,
  -- category_id: only overwrite when current value is still the default
  category_id          = CASE WHEN products.category_id = $2::uuid
                               THEN EXCLUDED.category_id ELSE products.category_id END,
  -- subcategory_id: COALESCE — don't erase an existing value if AI returned null
  subcategory_id       = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.subcategory_id, products.subcategory_id),
  -- AI SEO fields: COALESCE preserves existing values when AI was skipped
  seo_title            = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.seo_title,       products.seo_title),
  seo_description      = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.seo_description, products.seo_description),
  updated_at           = NOW()
RETURNING id, slug, (xmax = 0) AS was_inserted;

nodes.name: Upsert Product
nodes.notes: v2: All 34 DB columns mapped correctly. pros/cons/features/tags → text[] via jsonb_array_elements_text. subcategory_id (uuid). seo_og_image (jsonb). COALESCE on AI fields preserves existing data when AI is skipped.

nodes.name: Audit Log
nodes.notes: NEW: Structured audit log entry after every upsert. Extend to POST to a webhook / Datadog / Slack.

nodes.name: Throttle
nodes.notes: Configurable gap between ASIN iterations → back to Loop Over Items.

nodes.name: AI Rate Limit (Enrichment)
nodes.notes: v3: Proactively spaces OpenRouter requests by aiRateLimitMs (Config). Sits before the AI enrichment call. retryOnFail on the HTTP node still handles 429/transient errors reactively.

nodes.name: AI Rate Limit (Classifier)
nodes.notes: v3: Proactively spaces the second OpenRouter request (category classifier) by aiRateLimitMs (Config).
